text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Disability Associations 4 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK BEND OFFICE COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Educates members of the medical community, professionals, self-advocates, caregivers, and anyone in the greater community who is interested in making their home or place of business more welcoming for people living with disabilities. The program can be tailored to agency or group needs. Classes combine the real life expertise of families with accurate information on disability. The length of training can vary based on need. Full day trainings are also available. Topics that have been covered include, Creating a Welcoming Environment, Individual Education Plans (IEP), Fine Motor, Speech and Language, Financial Planning for People with Disabilities, Person Centered Planning, and Healthy Eating Habits and Special Diets. All classes provide families with free on-site child care. 2525 NE Twin Knolls Drive Suite 7 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm. Hours vary for classes. Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON ASSOCIATION OF AREA AGENCIES ON AGING AND DISABILITIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Oregon Association of Area Agencies on Aging and Disabilities (O4AD) is comprised of the 18 Area Agencies on Aging (AAA) located throughout Oregon. O4AD does not provide direct services to the public, but advocates for programs, services, funding, and strategies to serve older adults and people with disabilities. Community services funded by the Older Americans Act and home and community based services, such as Oregon Project Independence, are managed by local AAAs. AAAs provide case management for older adults and/or people with disabilities who are Medicaid clients in most urban areas of the state. Remaining areas are covered by Oregon Department of Human Services (ODHS) staff.
